definition: The process of identifying explaining correcting and verifying failures in computational systems.
A mismatch between expected behavior and observed behavior.
A record of execution path inputs states intermediate values or errors.
The ability to make a failure happen again under controlled conditions.
A proposed explanation for a failure.
Reduction of possible causes through smaller cases targeted tests or controlled inspection.
A test that preserves a known bug so it does not return unnoticed.
The ability to inspect internal behavior through logs traces metrics assertions and reports.
The likelihood that a failure will return or remain poorly understood.
